summary: Full e2e validation of Invoice Overlay Printer (no auth utility app). Backend pytest suite (7 tests) all pass: defaults endpoint, GET defaults when DB empty, POST persistence, /settings/reset, /generate-pdf returns valid PDF (Content-Type, magic bytes, size), generate-pdf with empty data, generate-pdf with inline settings override. Frontend: Fill Invoice page renders all 5 input groups (Header, PAN Numbers, Transport, Bottom Block, Totals) plus Bill To/Ship To and line-items table; Add Row / Remove Row work; Same as Bill To checkbox disables ship_to and mirrors bill_to value; Generate PDF triggers a real download named invoice_<inv_no>.pdf. Settings page loads with correct defaults (po_no.top=7.5, invoice_no.left=14.2); changing po_no.top to 8.5 + Save returns 200, GET /api/settings + UI reload both show the persisted 8.5; Reset to Defaults restores 7.5.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: SettingsPage NumberInput uses parseFloat(e.target.value) || 0 — typing '0' or clearing the field falls back to 0 which can confuse users. Consider allowing empty string state with validation on save.

Minor: backend /settings response includes the 'key' field when reading from DB but DEFAULT_SETTINGS also exposes it — frontend ignores it but the Settings Pydantic model uses extra='ignore' so harmless. Consider stripping it in get_settings for symmetry.
Nit: server.py has @app.on_event('shutdown') (deprecated in newer FastAPI) — works but lifespan handlers are preferred.
Good: every interactive element has data-testid; reportlab PDF generation is async-safe (CPU-bound but small). MongoDB ObjectId is correctly excluded via projection {'_id': 0}.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None. Tests reset DB state via /api/settings/reset before each backend test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all 4 endpoints with create→GET-verify pattern. Frontend has solid data-testid coverage. Note: page.fill() on type=number inputs in Settings page can produce odd states like '08.5' visually — prefer triple-click + Delete + type for number inputs in future Playwright runs (still results in correct numeric state via parseFloat).
